primary_accounting_issue: Identification of the hedge accounting models that can be documented in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what exposures can be designated as hedged items in hedge accounting, including the specific consolidation exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items and highly probable forecast intragroup transactions.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify the distinct hedge accounting model for a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation within a group context.
These paragraphs support the identification of hedge accounting as the governing framework and confirm that forecast transactions and aggregated exposures can be hedged items.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address presentation of dividends and related gains or losses, not the identification of hedge accounting models for FX risk in consolidation.
These paragraphs address profit or loss category classification for derivatives rather than which hedge accounting models are available.
These paragraphs relate to dividend disclosures and do not help identify hedge accounting approach families.
This Interpretation deals with distributions of non-cash assets to owners, not hedge accounting for FX on intragroup balances.
These paragraphs address classification of members' shares and related distributions, not consolidation hedge accounting.
These paragraphs concern statement of cash flows classification, not the hedge accounting model selection.
These paragraphs concern group and investee disclosures rather than hedge accounting eligibility or models.
These paragraphs address provisions and scope exclusions and are unrelated to hedge accounting for FX on intragroup dividends.
This Interpretation addresses debt-for-equity extinguishments, not hedge accounting in consolidation.
These paragraphs concern tax consequences of changes in tax status and are not relevant to identifying hedge accounting approaches.

treatment_families.family: General hedge accounting models available under IFRS 9 for eligible hedged items
Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements on intragroup items and transactions
Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting for recognised assets and liabilities as a top-level model, which can include eligible foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting for forecast transactions as a separate top-level model, and specifically permits certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to be hedged in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model within consolidated financial statements.
